project: Ramayana PS5 — ELGODS Sundara Kanda moment corpus
designPillars: 10 Sundara Kanda moments derived from ELGODS source citations
Valmiki sarga citations preserved per moment
Voice cue IDs follow Day 13 register pattern vc_sundara_<verb>_<id>

moments.narrative: Rama meets Hanuman in the Rishyamooka hills. The vanara is wild, loud, and loyal. When Hanuman sees Rama, something breaks open inside him. I am yours. The alliance that will move mountains is sealed in one glance.
moments.moralLesson: Recognition of the divine can come in a single glance

moments.narrative: Hanuman grows to mountain size. He crouches on Mainaka and launches himself across the southern ocean. Surasa opens her mouth to swallow him; he shrinks and enters, then expands and escapes. Sinhika tries to grab his shadow; he cuts her in half. The waves bow. Lanka sees his shadow and trembles.
moments.moralLesson: A single leap of faith can bridge an ocean

moments.narrative: The war is over. Rama stands before Sita in the field of Lanka. His eyes are cold. Let Agni, the fire, be the witness of your purity. Sita walks into the flames. The fire does not burn her. The gods witness: she is untouched.
moments.moralLesson: Purity tested by fire becomes light for the world

moments.narrative: Trijata, the rakshasi who dreams true, visits Sita in the Ashoka grove. I have seen Rama with an army of monkeys. I have seen Ravana fall. Lanka will burn. Wait, Mother. Victory is closer than tomorrow.

The rakshasas set his tail on fire. Hanuman leaps rooftop to rooftop across Lanka. Each touch ignites a demonstration of power. By dawn, Lanka is a lesson written in flame. Not out of rage — out of necessity.
moments.moralLesson: Even destruction can be an act of justice

moments.narrative: After the fire, Sita turns to the earth — her mother, Bhumi. I call upon you. Take me home. The ground splits open. A great throne rises. She descends into the earth that bore her. No argument. No doubt. She is claimed.

Ravana falls. The ten-headed tyrant who kidnapped dharma itself is defeated by the arrow of dharma. Rama does not gloat. He steps over the body and walks to the Ashoka grove. The war is over. The real victory is witnessing Sita walk out alive.
moments.moralLesson: True victory is measured by what is restored, not what is destroyed

moments.narrative: Mandodari stands before Ravana in the war chamber. She pleads: return Sita. Let dharma save Lanka. He laughs: I am Ravana. She turns away. Her silence is louder than his drums. The last voice of reason dies in the palace.
moments.moralLesson: A king deaf to wisdom dooms his own house

moments.narrative: Rama is crowned king of Ayodhya. Bharata places the crown upon his head. Sita stands beside him. The city is lit with a million lamps. The fourteen years are over. Ram Rajya begins — an age of justice, prosperity, and peace.
moments.moralLesson: The return home can be as holy as the quest itself

moments.narrative: Rama offers Hanuman any reward. I ask only to remain your servant. Wherever your name is spoken, there I will be. Hanuman becomes the symbol of selfless service that outlives kings, cities, and ages.
moments.moralLesson: Service without expectation is the highest reward
